Description: The "Confirm" button was clicked, the value will contain the result
The "Deny" button was clicked, the value will be false. Alternatively, if there's an input in a popup, you can use returnInputValueOnDeny: true to return the input's value.
The "Cancel" button was clicked, the dismiss will beSwal.DismissReason.cancel
The value from the popup, possible values:true for simple confirmed dialogsfalse for denied popupsany value for popups with inputs
The dismissal reason, see the Handling Dismissals section for details
